lean_stdlib_notes.nat_add_mod: Nat.add_mod (a b n : Nat) : (a + b) % n = (a % n + b % n) % n
lean_stdlib_notes.nat_mod_add_mod: Nat.mod_add_mod (m n k : Nat) : (m % n + k) % n = (m + k) % n
lean_stdlib_notes.nat_add_mod_right: Nat.add_mod_right (x z : Nat) : (x + z) % z = x % z
lean_stdlib_notes.nat_mod_eq_of_lt: Nat.mod_eq_of_lt : a < b → a % b = a
lean_stdlib_notes.no_split_ifs: split_ifs is a Mathlib tactic, NOT available in stdlib. Use by_cases instead.
lean_stdlib_notes.by_cases_usage: by_cases h : P <;> simp [h] works well for if-then-else goals
lean_stdlib_notes.strong_induction: Use 'induction n using Nat.strongRecOn with | ind n ih => ...' for strong induction
lean_stdlib_notes.no_push_neg: push_neg is Mathlib only. Use omega or Nat.lt_of_not_le instead.
lean_stdlib_notes.no_not_forall: not_forall simp lemma not in stdlib. Handle negated forall manually.
lean_stdlib_notes.eq_1_lemma: For recursive functions, use 'rw [f.eq_1]' to unfold one step without simp loops.
lean_stdlib_notes.if_neg_pattern: Use 'rw [if_neg h1, if_neg h2]' after 'rw [f.eq_1]' to unfold conditional branches.
lean_stdlib_notes.list_set_exists: List.set and List.length_set both exist in Lean 4.29 stdlib
lean_stdlib_notes.no_nat_decideGt_eq: Nat.decideGt_eq does NOT exist in Lean 4.28/4.29 stdlib
lean_stdlib_notes.no_bool_eq_false_iff_ne_true: Bool.eq_false_iff_ne_true does NOT exist; use: cases hb : b <;> simp_all
lean_stdlib_notes.no_prod_le: LE (Nat × Nat) not available in stdlib without Mathlib; use manual logGe def
lean_stdlib_notes.isUpToDate_iff: Use decide_eq_true_eq + beq_iff_eq for Bool/Nat comparison theorems
lean_stdlib_notes.no_list_get?: List.get? does NOT exist in Lean 4.29! Use l[i]? (List.getElem? notation)
lean_stdlib_notes.getElem?_eq_none: List.getElem?_eq_none : l.length ≤ i → l[i]? = none (direct function, NOT an Iff - do not use .mpr)
lean_stdlib_notes.getElem?_eq_getElem: List.getElem?_eq_getElem : n < l.length → l[n]? = some l[n]
lean_stdlib_notes.rintro_rfl_issue: rintro ⟨rfl, rfl⟩ fails when LHS is a record field (not a free variable). Use 'intro ⟨he, hs⟩; simp [he, hs]' instead.
lean_stdlib_notes.if_pos_rw: Use 'unfold f; rw [if_pos h1, h2]; simp [h3]' for nested if-match in proofs
lean_stdlib_notes.length_pos_alternative: List.length_pos.mpr does NOT exist. Use cases h_eq : l with | nil => exact absurd h_eq h | cons _ _ => simp
lean_stdlib_notes.wf_match_cases: For wf proofs with match on Option field, use 'cases hsnap : u.snapshot with | none => simp | some p => obtain ⟨sidx, _⟩ := p; simp only [hsnap] at h ⊢; omega'
lean_stdlib_notes.List_mem_cons_self: List.mem_cons_self has FULLY IMPLICIT args in Lean 4.28: write List.mem_cons.mpr (Or.inl rfl) instead of List.mem_cons_self v vs
lean_stdlib_notes.inner_have_induction: In inner 'have' induction blocks, the IH picks up outer 'have' hypotheses. Use standalone top-level lemmas to avoid IH type mismatches.
lean_stdlib_notes.refine_prod_eq: refine ⟨?_, ?_, ?_⟩ does NOT work for product-type equality goals (Eq has no explicit fields). Use 'simp only [f, h1, h2, h3]' or compute component-by-component.
lean_stdlib_notes.simp_leaves_zero_plus: After 'simp only [if_neg h]' on a sum, Lean may leave '0 + X = 0' instead of 'X = 0'. Use 'omega' after IH to close.
